sources.code:
# Time:  O(n * n!)
# Space: O(n)

class Solution(object):
    # @param num, a list of integer
    # @return a list of lists of integers
    def permute(self, num):
        result = []
        used = [False] * len(num)
        self.permuteRecu(result, used, [], num)
        return result

    def permuteRecu(self, result, used, cur, num):
        if len(cur) == len(num):
            result.append(cur[:])
            return
        for i in xrange(len(num)):
            if not used[i]:
                used[i] = True
                cur.append(num[i])
                self.permuteRecu(result, used, cur, num)
                cur.pop()
                used[i] = False


# Time:  O(n^2 * n!)
# Space: O(n^2)
class Solution2(object):
    def permute(self, nums):
        """
        :type nums: List[int]
        :rtype: List[List[int]]
        """
        res = []
        self.dfs(nums, [], res)
        return res

    def dfs(self, nums, path, res):
        if not nums:
            res.append(path)

        for i in xrange(len(nums)):
            # e.g., [1, 2, 3]: 3! = 6 cases
            # idx -> nums, path
            # 0 -> [2, 3], [1] -> 0: [3], [1, 2] -> [], [1, 2, 3]
            #                  -> 1: [2], [1, 3] -> [], [1, 3, 2]
            #
            # 1 -> [1, 3], [2] -> 0: [3], [2, 1] -> [], [2, 1, 3]
            #                  -> 1: [1], [2, 3] -> [], [2, 3, 1]
            #
            # 2 -> [1, 2], [3] -> 0: [2], [3, 1] -> [], [3, 1, 2]
            #                  -> 1: [1], [3, 2] -> [], [3, 2, 1]
            self.dfs(nums[:i] + nums[i+1:], path + [nums[i]], res)

# Time:  O(n * n!)
# Space: O(n)

class Solution(object):
    # @param num, a list of integer
    # @return a list of lists of integers
    def permute(self, num):
        result = []
        used = [False] * len(num)
        self.permuteRecu(result, used, [], num)

    def permuteRecu(self, result, used, cur, num):
        if len(cur) == len(num):
            result.append(cur[:])
            return
        for i in xrange(len(num)):
            if not used[i]:
                used[i] = True
                cur.append(num[i])
                self.permuteRecu(result, used, cur, num)
                cur.pop()
                used[i] = False


# Time:  O(n^2 * n!)
# Space: O(n^2)
class Solution2(object):
    def permute(self, nums):
        """
        :type nums: List[int]
        :rtype: List[List[int]]
        """
        res = []
        self.dfs(nums, [], res)
        return res

    def dfs(self, nums, path, res):
        if not nums:
            res.append(path)

        for i in xrange(len(nums)):
            # e.g., [1, 2, 3]: 3! = 6 cases
            # idx -> nums, path
            # 0 -> [2, 3], [1] -> 0: [3], [1, 2] -> [], [1, 2, 3]
            #                  -> 1: [2], [1, 3] -> [], [1, 3, 2]
            #
            # 1 -> [1, 3], [2] -> 0: [3], [2, 1] -> [], [2, 1, 3]
            #                  -> 1: [1], [2, 3] -> [], [2, 3, 1]
            #
            # 2 -> [1, 2], [3] -> 0: [2], [3, 1] -> [], [3, 1, 2]
            #                  -> 1: [1], [3, 2] -> [], [3, 2, 1]
            self.dfs(nums[:i] + nums[i+1:], path + [nums[i]], res)

# Time:  O(n * n!)
# Space: O(n)

# [Reader note] Entry point
# Start from the core routine and keep this mental model in view: Each step chooses the next unused item for the growing arrangement.
class Solution(object):
    # @param num, a list of integer
    # @return a list of lists of integers
    def permute(self, num):
        # [Reader note] State setup
        # This setup stage creates the state needed for the invariant or partial result: Choice tracking, used-element bookkeeping, and exploring order-sensitive search trees.
        result = []
        used = [False] * len(num)
        self.permuteRecu(result, used, [], num)
        return result

    def permuteRecu(self, result, used, cur, num):
        if len(cur) == len(num):
            result.append(cur[:])
            return
        # [Reader note] Main pass
        # This is the main sweep where the implementation earns its efficiency instead of restarting work from scratch.
        for i in xrange(len(num)):
            # [Reader note] Decision point
            # Pay attention here: The search explodes quickly, so clean bookkeeping matters more than clever optimization.
            if not used[i]:
                used[i] = True
                cur.append(num[i])
                self.permuteRecu(result, used, cur, num)
                cur.pop()
                used[i] = False


# Time:  O(n^2 * n!)
# Space: O(n^2)
class Solution2(object):
    def permute(self, nums):
        """
        :type nums: List[int]
        :rtype: List[List[int]]
        """
        res = []
        self.dfs(nums, [], res)
        # [Reader note] Return
        # The final return exposes the result after the traversal and state updates have settled.
        return res

    def dfs(self, nums, path, res):
        if not nums:
            res.append(path)

        for i in xrange(len(nums)):
            # e.g., [1, 2, 3]: 3! = 6 cases
            # idx -> nums, path
            # 0 -> [2, 3], [1] -> 0: [3], [1, 2] -> [], [1, 2, 3]
            #                  -> 1: [2], [1, 3] -> [], [1, 3, 2]
            #
            # 1 -> [1, 3], [2] -> 0: [3], [2, 1] -> [], [2, 1, 3]
            #                  -> 1: [1], [2, 3] -> [], [2, 3, 1]
            #
            # 2 -> [1, 2], [3] -> 0: [2], [3, 1] -> [], [3, 1, 2]
            #                  -> 1: [1], [3, 2] -> [], [3, 2, 1]
            self.dfs(nums[:i] + nums[i+1:], path + [nums[i]], res)

sources.reviewedCode:
# [Reader note] Entry point
# Start from the core routine and keep this mental model in view: Each step chooses the next unused item for the growing arrangement.
class Solution:
    def permute(self, nums: List[int]) -> List[List[int]]:
        def dfs(i: int):
            if i >= n:
                ans.append(t[:])
                return
            # [Reader note] Main pass
            # This is the main sweep where the implementation earns its efficiency instead of restarting work from scratch.
            for j, x in enumerate(nums):
                # [Reader note] Decision point
                # Pay attention here: The search explodes quickly, so clean bookkeeping matters more than clever optimization.
                if not vis[j]:
                    vis[j] = True
                    t[i] = x
                    dfs(i + 1)
                    vis[j] = False

        # [Reader note] State setup
        # This setup stage creates the state needed for the invariant or partial result: Choice tracking, used-element bookkeeping, and exploring order-sensitive search trees.
        n = len(nums)
        vis = [False] * n
        t = [0] * n
        ans = []
        dfs(0)
        # [Reader note] Return
        # The final return exposes the result after the traversal and state updates have settled.
        return ans

sources.reviewedCode:
# [Reader note] Entry point
# Start from the core routine and keep this mental model in view: Each step chooses the next unused item for the growing arrangement.
class Solution:
    def permute(self, nums):
        """
        :type nums: List[int]
        :rtype: List[List[int]]
        """
        def insert(P,k):
            # [Reader note] State setup
            # This setup stage creates the state needed for the invariant or partial result: Choice tracking, used-element bookkeeping, and exploring order-sensitive search trees.
            result = []
            # [Reader note] Main pass
            # This is the main sweep where the implementation earns its efficiency instead of restarting work from scratch.
            for p in P:
                for i in range(len(p)+1):
                    q = p[:i]+[k]+p[i:]
                    result.append(q)
            return result
        
        P = [[]]
        for i in nums:
            P = insert(P,i)
        # [Reader note] Return
        # The final return exposes the result after the traversal and state updates have settled.
        return P

writeups.title: permutations
writeups.excerpt: ## Prerequisites Before attempting this problem, you should be comfortable with: - **Recursion** - Building permutations by reducing to smaller subproblems - **Backtracking** - Exploring choices, then undoing them to try alternatives - **Arrays and List Operations** - Inserting elements at various positions and swapping - **Bit Manipulation (Optional)** - Using bitmasks to efficiently track used elements --- ## 1. R...

writeups.excerpt:
Recommended Time & Space Complexity

You should aim for a solution with O(n * n!) time and O(n) space, where n is the size of the input array.

writeups.title: 046.Permutations
writeups.excerpt: ### 046.Permutations 考虑将第n个数字，插入到已有的n-1个数字组成的全排列中去，注意有n个位置的插空。 [Leetcode Link](https://leetcode.com/problems/permutations)
